user_query:
I’m preparing a reproducible CI command plan for our .NET solution. Please read input/solution_spec.json to get the solution name, project paths, target framework, and package version. Then produce three files:

1) output/build_plan.md
- A concise plan with clearly separated stages: Restore, Build, Test, Publish, Pack, and Diagnostics.
- Use the exact solution/project paths from the input file.
- Include: a locked restore for the solution; a Release build with Deterministic=true, ContinuousIntegrationBuild=true, and parallelism; tests with TRX logger and results directory artifacts/testresults; a publish for the web project targeting linux-x64 that is self-contained and single-file with PublishDir=artifacts/publish/linux-x64/; pack the library project to artifacts/nuget/ using the version from the input.
- Include at least one binary log command.
- Use only dotnet msbuild (for restore/build/publish/pack) and dotnet test (for tests).
- Add a one-sentence rationale next to each command.

2) output/ci_commands.json
- A JSON array of the exact commands (strings) in the order they should be run in CI, with no comments.
- Use relative paths exactly as specified in input/solution_spec.json.
- Ensure the commands match the requirements above.

3) output/notes.txt
- 5–10 bullet points on trade-offs and cautions around single-file, self-contained, trimming (PublishTrimmed), and ReadyToRun. Note when to avoid trimming. Keep under 2500 characters.

Do not include placeholders. Use only relative workspace paths and only the input/ and output/ directories where applicable.
